query: Broccoli, some cashews, and a bunch of mint. Some Indian dish?

queries: Here is a great Indian-style broccoli and cashew curry recipe with fresh mint:
Here is a delicious Indian broccoli cashew stir-fry recipe using mint leaves:
Here is a flavorful Indian recipe combining broccoli, cashews, and mint chutney:

recommendation.headline: Malai Broccoli turns your broccoli, cashews, and mint into a creamy Indian appetizer, and it's the one recipe that actually calls for all three.
recommendation.why_it_fits: You've already got the core of this dish covered: broccoli, cashews, and mint are the backbone of the marinade and sauce. It's an appetizer format, so it comes together fast and doesn't need a long list of curry spices you might not have. The soaked cashews blend into the creamy base, so that jar of cashews does double duty as garnish and sauce.

missing_items.note: This is what makes the marinade creamy, plain yogurt works fine if you don't have cream.
Some versions use a bit of cheese in the coating for richness, skip it if you don't have any, the dish still works.

recommendation.how_to_use_sources: Follow the Malai Broccoli recipe on mygingergarlickitchen.com start to finish for the marinade, roasting or pan-searing, and the mint-cashew blend, it's built around exactly what you have.

recipe.title: Shahi Broccoli | Broccoli recipe Indian | how to make broccoli sabzi - Rumki's Golden Spoon

alternatives.reason: Pick the Shahi Broccoli recipe on rumkisgoldenspoon.com if you want a richer curry-style dish and don't mind leaving the mint out.
Go with the Broccoli-Cashew Poriyal on abitwholesomely.com if you'd rather do a quick South Indian stir-fry than a creamy appetizer.

query: Broccoli, cashews, and mint. One of those melt-in-your-mouth Indian restaurant starters?

queries: Here is a melt-in-your-mouth Indian restaurant-style broccoli cashew appetizer recipe:
Here is an Indian starter recipe using broccoli, cashews, and mint chutney:
Here is a creamy cashew broccoli Indian appetizer recipe with fresh mint:

recommendation.headline: Malai broccoli is exactly that melt-in-your-mouth Indian starter, and the Mygingergarlickitchen version already blends broccoli, cashews, and mint right into the marinade.
recommendation.why_it_fits: You named broccoli, cashews, and mint, and this is the one recipe on the list that actually works all three into the creamy marinade itself rather than just using mint as a garnish. The cashew-yogurt base is what gives malai broccoli that soft, rich, restaurant-style texture you're after. It's a starter built to be scooped up hot, no elaborate equipment needed.

missing_items.note: This thickens the marinade and balances the cashew richness, plain full fat yogurt works fine.
Adds extra creaminess but you can skip it and lean on the cashew paste alone.

missing_items.ingredient: ginger garlic paste and garam masala or similar spices
missing_items.note: These carry the savory backbone of the marinade, don't skip them even if you cut back on other extras.

recommendation.how_to_use_sources: Follow the Mygingergarlickitchen malai broccoli page for the full method and marinade ratios since it already folds mint into the cashew-yogurt mix. If you want a firmer, more charred tikka-style bite instead of the softer version, glance at the Air Fryer Malai Broccoli Tikka page on Yummy Tales of Tummy for its air-frying time and temperature.

recipe.title: Air Fryer Malai Broccoli - My Food Story

alternatives.reason: Pick the My Food Story kebab version if you want skewered, air-fried tikka texture with a trusted, detailed method.

recipe.title: Air Fryer Malai Broccoli Recipe  - Fun FOOD Frolic

alternatives.reason: Choose the Fun Food Frolic recipe if you have paneer or cheese on hand and want an even richer, blanched-first version.

recipe.title: Easy Creamy Malai Broccoli Kebab Recipe

alternatives.reason: Go with the Eat Healthy 365 kebab recipe if you're fine with a longer ingredient list and want a more elaborate cream cheese marinade.
